resources.string: Which keyword declares a variable that can change in modern JavaScript?
let declares a variable whose value can change.
Which keyword declares a constant in JavaScript?
const declares a reference that cannot be reassigned.
Which old keyword declares variables with function scope?
var declares function-scoped variables and is considered less recommended than let/const.
Which method prints to the console?
console.log() prints messages to the console.
Which operator compares value and type?
=== performs strict comparison: value and type.
Which operator compares equality with type conversion?
In JavaScript, numbers use the number type.
Which value represents an intentional absence of value?
null represents an intentional absence of value.
Which value appears when a variable has no assigned value?
undefined means no value has been assigned.
Which method converts JSON into an object?
JSON.parse() converts JSON text into an object.
Which method converts an object into JSON text?
JSON.stringify() converts objects into JSON strings.
Which method adds an element to the end of an array?
push() adds an element to the end of the array.
Which method removes the last element from an array?
pop() removes and returns the last element.
Which method removes the first element from an array?
Which method adds an element to the beginning of an array?
unshift() adds elements to the beginning.
Which method iterates over each element of an array?
forEach() runs an action for each element.
Which method transforms each element and returns a new array?
map() transforms each element and returns a new array.
Which method returns elements that meet a condition?
filter() filters elements based on a condition.
Which method returns the first element that meets a condition?
find() returns the first matching element.
Which method checks whether any element meets a condition?
some() returns true if any element meets the condition.
Which method checks whether all elements meet a condition?
every() requires all elements to meet the condition.
Which method joins array elements into text?
join() joins the elements into a string.
Which method checks whether an array contains a value?
includes() checks whether a value is in the array.
Which structure runs code if a condition is met?
if runs a block based on a condition.
Which structure lets you choose between several cases?
Which loop is normally used to repeat with a counter?
for is commonly used with counters.
Which loop repeats while a condition is true?
while repeats while the condition is true.
break interrupts a loop or switch.
Which word skips to the next iteration?
continue moves to the next iteration.
Which word declares a traditional function?
Which syntax represents an arrow function?
Which object represents the HTML document in the browser?
Which method selects the first element that matches a CSS selector?
querySelector() selects the first matching element.
Which method selects all elements that match a CSS selector?
querySelectorAll() returns a list of matches.
Which method listens for events on an element?
addEventListener() registers a function for an event.
Which event occurs when submitting a form?
submit fires when submitting a form.
Which event occurs when an input value changes while typing?
input fires when the value changes.
Which keyword is used to handle errors?
Which keyword throws an error manually?
Which object represents a future asynchronous operation?
Which keyword declares an asynchronous function?
Which keyword waits for the result of a Promise?
await waits for a Promise inside an async function.
Which method is used to consume a resolved Promise?
then() runs when a Promise is resolved.
Which method is used to catch Promise errors?
Which API is commonly used to make HTTP requests?
Which keyword calls the parent constructor in a child class?
super() calls the parent class constructor.
Which operator returns an alternative value if the left side is null or undefined?
let allows reassigning the value of a variable.
const allows freely reassigning a variable.
const does not allow reassigning the reference.
var has block scope just like let.
var has function scope, not block scope.
null and undefined are exactly the same.
JSON.parse() converts JSON text into an object.
JSON.stringify() converts an object into JSON text.
push() adds elements to the end of an array.
unshift() adds elements to the beginning.
map() always modifies the original array.
filter() returns elements that meet a condition.
forEach() returns a new transformed array.
forEach does not return a transformed array.
find() returns the first matching element.
some() checks whether any element meets a condition.
some returns true if one meets the condition.
every() requires all elements to meet a condition.
every requires all of them to meet the condition.
join() joins array elements into a string.
includes() checks whether an array contains a value.
if is used to run code based on a condition.
continue skips to the next iteration.
querySelector selects the first one; querySelectorAll selects all.
submit is used when submitting forms.
submit occurs when submitting a form.
try/catch is used to catch errors.
await can be used freely in any normal function.
await is normally used inside async functions or modules.
async turns a function into an asynchronous one.
async makes the function return a Promise.
fetch() is used to make HTTP requests.
fetch allows requesting resources over the network.
then() allows handling a resolved Promise.
class allows declaring classes in JavaScript.
super() can call the parent constructor.
this always points to the same object regardless of context.
this depends on the execution context.
NaN represents an invalid numeric result.
\?? returns the right-hand value if the left-hand value is null or undefined.
\?. allows safely accessing possibly null properties.
\?. avoids errors if the object is null or undefined.
let can declare a numeric variable.
The block is written between braces.
Complete the code:\n\nfor (let i = 0; i < 5; i____) { }
while runs a block between braces.
Complete the code:\n\nconst sumar = (a, b) ____ a + b;
pop removes and returns the last one.
Complete the code:\n\nconst dobles = numeros.____(n => n * 2);
Complete the code:\n\nconst pares = numeros.____(n => n % 2 === 0);
filter keeps the ones that meet the condition.
Complete the code:\n\nconst mayor = numeros.____(n => n > 10);
find returns the first matching element.
Complete the code:\n\nconst hayPares = numeros.____(n => n % 2 === 0);
some checks whether any item meets the condition.
Complete the code:\n\nconst todosPositivos = numeros.____(n => n > 0);
every checks whether all items meet the condition.
includes checks whether it contains a value.
JSON.parse converts JSON text into an object.
JSON.stringify converts an object into JSON text.
querySelector selects the first matching element.
submit can be listened for with addEventListener.
The parameter is assigned to the attribute.
\?? uses an alternative if there is null or undefined.
>= means greater than or equal to.
Number.parseInt converts text to an integer.
Object model of the HTML document
The DOM represents the structure of the HTML document as objects.
A callback is a function passed to another function.
Una Promise represents a future result.
Function that reacts to an event
Syntax for working with asynchronous operations
async/await makes working with Promises easier.
Function that remembers variables from its context
A closure keeps access to external variables.
Hoisting conceptually moves certain declarations to the beginning.
Falsy is a value treated as false in conditions.
Template literals use backticks and allow ${}.
Extract values from objects or arrays
Assignment is used instead of comparison
const prevents reassignment, but not modifying the array.
Accessing a property of null causes an error.
After return, the code is not executed.
JSON keys must use double quotes.
The same identifier cannot be declared twice in the same scope
You cannot redeclare the same name in the same scope.
await needs an async context or module
await is used inside async functions or modules.
push adds an element and length becomes 3.
Order the code to declare a variable and display it:
First it is declared, then it is used.
Order the code to add two numbers:
First values, then calculation, then output.
Order the code to recorrer un array:
Order the code to transformar un array:
Order the code to usar async/await:
Order the code to escuchar un click:
First you select, then you add the listener.
Order the code to filtrar mayores de edad:
Order the code to parsear JSON:
Order the code to capturar un error:
Order the code to crear una clase:
A class contains a constructor and assignments.
3-letter word that declares a modern mutable variable.
5-letter word that declares a non-reassignable reference.
5-letter word used to wait for a promise.
5-letter word used to declare a class.
Concept of an asynchronous operation that is resolved or rejected.
3-letter word that represents the web document as objects.
JSON is widely used to exchange data.
Word used to call the parent constructor.
Clue: method to print to the console.
Clue: HTML structure represented as objects.
Clue: data format used in APIs.
JSON is a data exchange format.
Clue: function passed as an argument.
A callback is a function passed as an argument.
Clue: word for an asynchronous function.
Clue: word to wait for a Promise.
Clue: method to select an element.
querySelector selects an element from the DOM.
Clue: method to listen for events.
